chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2662 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ هَارُونَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَكَلْتُ خَبِيصاً فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ حَتَّى شَبِعْتُ مِنْهُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «إِذَا فَرَغْتَ مِنْ مَنَاسِكِكَ وَ أَرَدْتَ اَلْخُرُوجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَابْتَعْ بِدِرْهَمٍ تَمْراً وَ تَصَدَّقْ بِهِ فَيَكُونَ كَفَّارَةً لِذَلِكَ وَ لِمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْكَ فِي إِحْرَامِكَ مِمَّا لاَ تَعْلَمُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2662 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Harun, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I ate a sweet dish (khabis) that contained saffron until I was full while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "When you complete your rituals and wish to leave Makkah, buy dates with a dirham and give them in charity. This will be an expiation for that and for anything else that might have affected your Ihram unknowingly."
